prompt:
Security asked for a quarterly inventory of every secret's lifecycle metadata across our vaults — what's enabled, what expires when, who owns what (tags) — and explicitly: the report must never contain or even fetch a secret VALUE. Our little kvclient.py session (bearer auth + JSON GETs + a get_secret used by deploy jobs) works fine and must keep working; what's missing is the inventory layer.

Start with research, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Azure Key Vault REST reference — at minimum the Get Secrets (list) operation page and the Get Deleted Secrets page — plus Microsoft's Key Vault throttling and authentication guidance. Watch the api-version story carefully: I've seen 7.x in old scripts and something date-shaped in newer docs, and I want the version we pin to be whatever the current GA reference actually says, with the discrepancy called out if there is one. Only Microsoft's first-party pages count as sources. docs/contract.json and docs/official_sources.json hold our pinned contract and provenance; both are protected — if the live docs disagree with them, report the conflict in your summary instead of editing anything protected. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

What I need, in a new kvinventory.py (the acceptance tests import it):

- collect_inventory(session, include_deleted=False, max_page_size=None) returning {"vault", "active", "deleted"}. Active entries carry name (from the id URL), id, enabled, created, updated, not_before, expires, tags, content_type, managed — missing attributes come through as None (tags as {}), nothing invented. Deleted entries additionally carry recovery_id, deleted_date, scheduled_purge_date and only exist when include_deleted=True (don't even hit that endpoint otherwise).
- Paging done the way the docs define it: request the list endpoint with the researched api-version (and maxresults only when a page size was asked for), then follow nextLink EXACTLY as the service hands it back — no rebuilding, no query merging, no "fixing" its api-version — until there is no link. An empty page with a link is not the end. Every request, including link follows, authenticates through the session.
- Never touch /secrets/{name} — metadata only.
- Error mapping with distinct exception types: VaultRequestError base carrying status/code/message parsed from the documented Key Vault error body, with VaultAuthenticationError for 401, VaultAuthorizationError for 403, and VaultThrottledError for 429 (carrying retry_after from the Retry-After header, None when absent). A 500 stays the plain base type. Errors mid-pagination map the same way.

Acceptance: python3 test_inventory.py — it runs a loopback mock vault, so no Azure account and no real tokens are involved. test_inventory.py and everything under docs/ are protected; kvclient.py's existing behavior (get_secret included) has to keep passing.

research.interface: Key Vault REST API api-version 2025-07-01: Get Secrets and Get Deleted Secrets list operations

official_sources.title: Get Secrets - Get Secrets - REST API (Azure Key Vault)
official_sources.used_for: GET {vaultBaseUrl}/secrets?api-version=2025-07-01 (the current default moniker), maxresults 1-25, SecretListResult{value, nextLink}, SecretItem{id, attributes, tags, contentType, managed}, SecretAttributes{enabled, created, updated, nbf, exp, recoveryLevel, recoverableDays}, the KeyVaultError{error{code, message, innererror}} shape, and the sample response whose nextLink carries a different api-version (7.2) proving the link is opaque

official_sources.title: Get Deleted Secrets - Get Deleted Secrets - REST API (Azure Key Vault)
official_sources.used_for: GET {vaultBaseUrl}/deletedsecrets?api-version=2025-07-01, DeletedSecretListResult paging, and DeletedSecretItem's recoveryId/deletedDate/scheduledPurgeDate fields on top of the SecretItem shape (id keeps pointing at /secrets/{name})

official_sources.title: Azure Key Vault throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: HTTP 429 as the throttle signal, the exponential backoff recommendation, and the note that throttled requests do not count against vault limits

official_sources.title: Authenticate to Azure Key Vault
official_sources.used_for: bearer-token authentication on every data-plane call, 401 challenge behavior for missing/invalid tokens, and the forbidden (403) response for firewall blocks or missing secrets/list permission

research.language_rationale: Python is the language of the existing vault tooling (kvclient.py). The inventory is built on the documented REST contract through the stdlib urllib stack the module already uses: azure-keyvault-secrets would add azure-core/azure-identity dependencies only to re-expose the same list operations, and the task's core behaviors (opaque nextLink following, distinct 401/403/429 mapping) are REST-level semantics the SDK would hide.
